2026-04-18T17:19:12.1101815Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-18T17:19:12.1102734Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-18T17:19:12.1103187Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-18T17:19:12.1103556Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-18T17:19:12.1103860Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-18T17:19:12.1104094Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-18T17:19:12.1104248Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m
2026-04-18T17:19:12.1104424Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-18T17:19:12.1104593Z [36;1m# Set outputs for next steps[0m
2026-04-18T17:19:12.1104822Z [36;1m{[0m
2026-04-18T17:19:12.1105028Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_api_key=${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-18T17:19:12.1105317Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_port=${PORT}"[0m
2026-04-18T17:19:12.1105571Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

Supports reply_to_id for discussion threading.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.","type":["number","string"]},"reply_to_id":{"description":"Node ID of the discussion comment to reply to, enabling threaded discussion comments. 2026-04-18T17:21:42.7845125Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-18T17:20:14.578458347Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58328,\"title\":\"[Storage] Docs: Document snapshot and versioning transfer support in Azure.Storage.DataMovement.Blobs README\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `Azure.Storage.DataMovement.Blobs`\\n**Service directory:** `sdk/storage/Azure.Storage.DataMovement.Blobs/`\\n**Triggered by:** commit [REDACTED] (PR #57368) by `@amnguye`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nPR #57368 added two new properties to `BlobStorageResourceOptions`:\\n- `Snapshot` — specifies a snapshot identifier when using a blob snapshot as the source\\n- `VersionId` — specifies a version identifier when using a blob version as the source\\n\\nThese allow callers to copy/download a specific blob snapshot or version, with full pause/resume (checkpoint) support. The two properties are mutually exclusive; setting both throws an exception.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- No README section or example demonstrating how to transfer from a blob snapshot using `BlobStorageResourceOptions.Snapshot`\\n- No README section or example demonstrating how to transfer from a blob version using `BlobStorageResourceOptions.VersionId`\\n- No sample test file exists under `sdk/storage/Azure.Storage.DataMovement.Blobs/tests/Samples/` for either feature\\n- The `Examples` section in the README makes no mention of snapshots or versioning, leaving users unaware of these new capabilities introduced in 12.4.0-beta.1\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\nNo existing files need to be removed. Two actions are required:\\n1. Create a new sample test file\\n2. Add snippet placeholders and prose to the existing README\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nCreate a new test sample file at `sdk/storage/Azure.Storage.DataMovement.Blobs/tests/Samples/Sample_SnapshotVersionTransfer.cs` with the following content:\\n\\n```csharp\\n// Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved.\\n// Licensed under the MIT License.\\n\\nusing System;\\nusing System.Threading.Tasks;\\nusing Azure.Core;\\nusing Azure.Storage.DataMovement;\\nusing Azure.Storage.DataMovement.Blobs;\\nusing NUnit.Framework;\\n\\nnamespace Azure.Storage.DataMovement.Blobs.Samples\\n{\\n    public class Sample_SnapshotVersionTransfer : SamplesBase\\n    {\\n        [Test]\\n        [LiveOnly]\\n        public async Task TransferFromSnapshotAsync()\\n        {\\n            TokenCredential tokenCredential = new DefaultAzureCredential();\\n            BlobsStorageResourceProvider blobs = new(tokenCredential);\\n            TransferManager transferManager = new TransferManager();\\n\\n            Uri sourceBlobUri = new Uri(\\u0026#34;(myaccount.blob.core.windows.net/redacted)\\n            string snapshotId = \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;;  // e.g. \\u0026#34;2023-01-01T00:00:00.0000000Z\\u0026#34;\\n            Uri destinationBlobUri = new Uri(\\u0026#34;(myaccount.blob.core.windows.net/redacted)\\n\\n            #region Snippet:TransferFromSnapshot\\n            // Download or copy a blob snapshot by specifying the snapshot identifier\\n            // in the source resource options.\\n            StorageResource sourceSnapshot = await blobs.FromBlobAsync(\\n                sourceBlobUri,\\n                new BlockBlobStorageResourceOptions\\n                {\\n                    Snapshot = snapshotId\\n                });\\n\\n            StorageResource destination = await blobs.FromBlobAsync(destinationBlobUri);\\n\\n            TransferOperation transferOperation = await transferManager.StartTransferAsync(\\n                sourceResource: sourceSnapshot,\\n                destinationResource: destination);\\n            await transferOperation.WaitForCompletionAsync();\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n\\n        [Test]\\n        [LiveOnly]\\n        public async Task TransferFromVersionAsync()\\n        {\\n            TokenCredential tokenCredential = new DefaultAzureCredential();\\n            BlobsStorageResourceProvider blobs = new(tokenCredential);\\n            TransferManager transferManager = new TransferManager();\\n\\n            Uri sourceBlobUri = new Uri(\\u0026#34;(myaccount.blob.core.windows.net/redacted)\\n            string versionId = \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;;\\n            Uri destinationBlobUri = new Uri(\\u0026#34;(myaccount.blob.core.windows.net/redacted)\\n\\n            #region Snippet:TransferFromVersion\\n            // Download or copy a specific blob version by specifying the version identifier\\n            // in the source resource options.\\n            StorageResource sourceVersion = await blobs.FromBlobAsync(\\n                sourceBlobUri,\\n                new BlockBlobStorageResourceOptions\\n                {\\n                    VersionId = versionId\\n                });\\n\\n            StorageResource destination = await blobs.FromBlobAsync(destinationBlobUri);\\n\\n            TransferOperation transferOperation = await transferManager.StartTransferAsync(\\n                sourceResource: sourceVersion,\\n                destinationResource: destination);\\n            await transferOperation.WaitForCompletionAsync();\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\n```\\n\\nFor each snippet, the details are:\\n- **Snippet `TransferFromSnapshot`**: test file path above; README location → after the existing `Blob Copy` section, in a new `### Copy or Download from a Snapshot` subsection\\n- **Snippet `TransferFromVersion`**: test file path above; README location → after the snapshot subsection, in a new `### Copy or Download from a Version` subsection\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README at `sdk/storage/Azure.Storage.DataMovement.Blobs/README.md` currently has all required top-level sections (Getting started, Key concepts, Examples, Troubleshooting, Next steps, Contributing). Only the **Examples** section needs new subsections.\\n\\nAdd the following two subsections to `README.md` inside the **Examples** section, after the existing `### Blob Copy` subsection (around line 229):\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Copy or Download from a Snapshot\\n\\nA blob snapshot transfer uses a specific snapshot as the source. Set the `Snapshot` property on the source resource options with the snapshot identifier.\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\n\\u0026gt; **Note:** `Snapshot` and `VersionId` are mutually exclusive; setting both will throw an `ArgumentException`.\\n\\n### Copy or Download from a Version\\n\\nA blob version transfer uses a specific version as the source. Set the `VersionId` property on the source resource options with the version identifier.\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\n\\u0026gt; **Note:** `VersionId` and `Snapshot` are mutually exclusive; setting both will throw an `ArgumentException`.\\n```\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order. Each must succeed before proceeding to the next.\\n\\n1. `dotnet build sdk/storage/Azure.Storage.DataMovement.Blobs/`\\n2. `dotnet test sdk/storage/Azure.Storage.DataMovement.Blobs/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 storage` — injects snippet code from test file into README.md placeholders; verify the README diff looks correct\\n4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 storage` — only needed if public types changed; the `Snapshot` and `VersionId` properties were already exported in this PR, so verify the `.api.cs` files haven\\u0026#39;t changed\\n5. `dotnet format sdk/storage/Azure.Storage.DataMovement.Blobs/`\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt; [!TIP]\\n\\u0026gt; **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\\u0026gt; - `pkgs.dev.azure.com`\\n\\u0026gt; - `releaseassets.githubusercontent.com`\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; To allow these domains, add them to the `network.allowed` list in your workflow frontmatter:\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; ```yaml\\n\\u0026gt; network:\\n\\u0026gt;   allowed:\\n\\u0026gt;     - defaults\\n\\u0026gt;     - \\u0026#34;pkgs.dev.azure.com\\u0026#34;\\n\\u0026gt;     - \\u0026#34;releaseassets.githubusercontent.com\\u0026#34;\\n\\u0026gt; TokenCredential of AddBlobServiceClient(...) is always DefaultAzureCredential\",\"body\":\"### Library name and version\\n\\nMicrosoft.Extensions.Azure 1.11.0\\n\\n### Describe the bug\\n\\nWithin the `BlobClientBuilderExtensions` the method\\n\\n```C#\\npublic static IAzureClientBuilder\\u003cBlobServiceClient, BlobClientOptions\\u003e AddBlobServiceClient\\u003cTBuilder\\u003e(this TBuilder builder, Uri serviceUri, TokenCredential tokenCredential)\\n    where TBuilder : IAzureClientFactoryBuilderWithCredential\\n{\\n    return builder.RegisterClientFactory\\u003cBlobServiceClient, BlobClientOptions\\u003e((options, token) =\\u003e new BlobServiceClient(serviceUri, token, options));\\n}\\n```\\nis not passing down the provided `TokenCredential`.\\n\\n### Expected behavior\\n\\nThe provided `TokenCredential` will be used, maybe like this:\\n\\n```C#\\npublic static IAzureClientBuilder\\u003cBlobServiceClient, BlobClientOptions\\u003e AddBlobServiceClient\\u003cTBuilder\\u003e(this TBuilder builder, Uri serviceUri, TokenCredential tokenCredential)\\n    where TBuilder : IAzureClientFactoryBuilderWithCredential\\n{\\n    return builder.RegisterClientFactory\\u003cBlobServiceClient, BlobClientOptions\\u003e((options, _) =\\u003e new BlobServiceClient(serviceUri, tokenCredential, options));\\n}\\n```\\n\\n### Actual behavior\\n\\nThe `DefaultAzureCredential` is always being used, which leads to unnecessary token requests, that mostly cannot be resolved and even throw exceptions when some configuration is missing.\\n\\n### Reproduction Steps\\n\\nAdd the service to the `IServiceCollection` like this:\\n\\n```C#\\nservices.AddAzureClients(\\n    config =\\u003e\\n    {\\n        config.AddBlobServiceClient(\\n            new Uri(\\\"\\u003curl to the storage account\\u003e\\\"),\\n            new AzureCliCredential());\\n    });\\n```\\n\\n### Environment\\n\\n_No
